GT LEARN #763 ✂️ Partition Labels MEDIUM Greedy Two Pointers Hash Map
s= exp: [9,7,8]
📝 Problem Statement
Given a string s, partition it into as many parts as possible so that each letter appears in at most one part. Return a list of the lengths of these parts.
🧠 Solving Approach
Greedy + last-occurrence map:
1. First pass: record lastIdx[ch] = the rightmost index where each char appears.
2. Second pass: walk left→right, maintaining a window [start, end]. At each i, extend end = max(end, lastIdx[s[i]]). When i == end, every char inside the window has its last occurrence inside too — safe to cut! Append length, slide start = i+1.
🎯 Core Concepts
Last-occurrence map: for each letter, the highest index it appears at. Pre-computed in O(n).
Greedy window: end is the furthest we must reach to honor all letters seen so far. We can't cut earlier without splitting some letter.
Cut condition: i == end means current position has caught up to the furthest required reach — no letter inside this window appears outside it.
⏱ Time: O(n) 💾 Space: O(1) — at most 26 letters
🧪 Test Cases (click to load)
CLASSICLC official example→ [9,7,8]
"ababcbacadefegdehijhklij"
SINGLEone big chunk→ [10]
"eccbbbbdec"
DISTINCTevery letter unique→ [1,1,1,1,1,1]
"abcdef"
SINGLE-CHedge case→ [1]
"a"
TRICKYsmall + big mix→ [1,9]
"caedbdedda"
Result: —
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization — String, Window & Cuts
0/0
←→R 100%
